function_explanation: Generates a 2D array representing a filled ellipse with a specified intensity value on a given coordinate grid.
function_docstring:
Generates a single ellipse mask scaled by gray_level.

Args:
    x_grid (numpy.ndarray): A 2D array of shape (H, W) representing the x-coordinates of the grid.
    y_grid (numpy.ndarray): A 2D array of shape (H, W) representing the y-coordinates of the grid.
    x0 (float): The x-coordinate of the ellipse center.
    y0 (float): The y-coordinate of the ellipse center.
    a (float): The length of the semi-major axis (along the x-axis before rotation).
    b (float): The length of the semi-minor axis (along the y-axis before rotation).
    gray_level (float): The value assigned to the pixels inside the ellipse.
    theta (float, optional): The rotation angle of the ellipse in radians. Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    numpy.ndarray: A 2D array of shape (H, W) containing the generated ellipse mask multiplied by the gray level.
usage_example:
import numpy as np

# Create a coordinate grid
x = np.linspace(-10, 10, 200)
y = np.linspace(-10, 10, 200)
x_grid, y_grid = np.meshgrid(x, y)

# Generate a rotated ellipse
ellipse_image = _gen_ellipse(
    x_grid=x_grid,
    y_grid=y_grid,
    x0=0.0,
    y0=0.0,
    a=5.0,
    b=2.0,
    gray_level=1.0,
    theta=np.pi / 4
)
